title: RiemannianInvariant
summary: Defines symbolic scalar/tensor Riemannian invariants with rank, conformal weight, tensor weight, and parity bookkeeping.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines symbolic scalar/tensor Riemannian invariants with rank, conformal weight, tensor weight, and parity bookkeeping.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Invariants/RiemannianInvariant.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Orientation parity of a symbolic invariant. Its constructors are: even; odd; mixed.
Parity of a tensor product. In explicit terms, its defining expression is | even, p maps to p | p, even maps to p | odd, odd maps to even | mixed, maps to mixed |, mixed maps to mixed.
Symbolic data describing a partial contraction of tensor indices. Its mathematical data are: pairs: List Nat × Nat; validity: Prop.
Number of contracted index pairs. In explicit terms, for c of type index pairing, its defining expression is C.pairs.length.

Syntax for natural tensor invariants and the operations used in the paper. Its constructors are: const One; metric; inv Metric; riem; cov Deriv Riem with arguments order; tensor Prod with arguments A B : Tensor Invariant Expr; contract with arguments A : Tensor Invariant Expr C : Index Pairing; lin Comb with arguments terms : List Rat × Tensor Invariant Expr; scalar Mul with arguments c : Rat A : Tensor Invariant Expr; add with arguments A B : Tensor Invariant Expr; laplacian with arguments A : Tensor Invariant Expr; j Mul with arguments A : Tensor Invariant Expr; divergence with arguments A : Tensor Invariant Expr; ambient Laplacian with arguments A : Tensor Invariant Expr; restrict Ambient with arguments A : Tensor Invariant Expr; pf Like with arguments order A : Tensor Invariant Expr.
A base Riemannian invariant with formal metadata. Its mathematical data are: expr: Tensor Invariant Expr; rank: Nat; weight: Int; parity: Invariant Parity.
An ambient Riemannian invariant with formal metadata. Its mathematical data are: expr: Tensor Invariant Expr; rank: Nat; weight: Int; parity: Invariant Parity.
Scalar base invariants. In explicit terms, its defining expression is I : Tensor Invariant divided by divided by I.rank equals 0.
Rank-one base invariants. In explicit terms, its defining expression is I : Tensor Invariant divided by divided by I.rank equals 1.
Scalar ambient invariants. In explicit terms, its defining expression is I : Ambient Invariant divided by divided by I.rank equals 0.
Rank-one ambient invariants. In explicit terms, its defining expression is I : Ambient Invariant divided by divided by I.rank equals 1.
Tensor weight weight - rank, invariant under contractions. In explicit terms, for i of type tensor invariant, its defining expression is I.weight minus I.rank.
Ambient tensor weight weight - rank. In explicit terms, for i of type ambient invariant, its defining expression is I.weight minus I.rank.

The constant scalar invariant 1. In explicit terms, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.const One rank : equals 0 weight : equals 0 parity : equals Invariant Parity.even, rfl ⟩.

The constant ambient scalar invariant 1. In explicit terms, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.const One rank : equals 0 weight : equals 0 parity : equals Invariant Parity.even, rfl ⟩.

Tensor product of base invariants. In explicit terms, for s, t of type tensor invariant, its defining expression is expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.tensor Prod S.expr T.expr rank : equals S.rank plus T.rank weight : equals S.weight plus T.weight parity : equals Invariant Parity.tensor Product S.parity T.parity.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{tensorProdInvariant}(S,T)=\operatorname{expr} = \operatorname{TensorInvariantExpr}.\operatorname{tensorProd} S.\operatorname{expr} T.\operatorname{expr} \operatorname{rank} = S.\operatorname{rank} + T.\operatorname{rank} \operatorname{weight} = S.\operatorname{weight} + T.\operatorname{weight} \operatorname{parity} = \operatorname{InvariantParity}.\operatorname{tensorProduct} S.\operatorname{parity} T.\operatorname{parity}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Tensor product of ambient invariants. In explicit terms, for s, t of type ambient invariant, its defining expression is expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.tensor Prod S.expr T.expr rank : equals S.rank plus T.rank weight : equals S.weight plus T.weight parity : equals Invariant Parity.tensor Product S.parity T.parity.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{ambientTensorProdInvariant}(S,T)=\operatorname{expr} = \operatorname{TensorInvariantExpr}.\operatorname{tensorProd} S.\operatorname{expr} T.\operatorname{expr} \operatorname{rank} = S.\operatorname{rank} + T.\operatorname{rank} \operatorname{weight} = S.\operatorname{weight} + T.\operatorname{weight} \operatorname{parity} = \operatorname{InvariantParity}.\operatorname{tensorProduct} S.\operatorname{parity} T.\operatorname{parity}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Partial contraction of a base tensor. Every contracted pair lowers both rank and metric weight by two, preserving tensor weight when enough indices are present. In explicit terms, for t of type tensor invariant, and c of type index pairing, its defining expression is expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.contract T.expr C rank : equals T.rank minus 2 times C.pair Count weight : equals T.weight minus 2 times C.pair Count parity : equals T.parity.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{contractInvariant}(T,C)=\operatorname{expr} = \operatorname{TensorInvariantExpr}.\operatorname{contract} T.\operatorname{expr} C \operatorname{rank} = T.\operatorname{rank} - 2 \cdot C.\operatorname{pairCount} \operatorname{weight} = T.\operatorname{weight} - 2 \cdot C.\operatorname{pairCount} \operatorname{parity} = T.\operatorname{parity}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Partial contraction of an ambient tensor. In explicit terms, for t of type ambient invariant, and c of type index pairing, its defining expression is expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.contract T.expr C rank : equals T.rank minus 2 times C.pair Count weight : equals T.weight minus 2 times C.pair Count parity : equals T.parity.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{ambientContractInvariant}(T,C)=\operatorname{expr} = \operatorname{TensorInvariantExpr}.\operatorname{contract} T.\operatorname{expr} C \operatorname{rank} = T.\operatorname{rank} - 2 \cdot C.\operatorname{pairCount} \operatorname{weight} = T.\operatorname{weight} - 2 \cdot C.\operatorname{pairCount} \operatorname{parity} = T.\operatorname{parity}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For T of type Tensor Invariant, C of type Index Pairing, and hC of type 2 times C.pair Count < equals T.rank, tensor Weight contract Invariant T C equals tensor Weight T.
correspondence.leanType: theorem tensorWeight_contractInvariant (T : TensorInvariant) (C : IndexPairing) (hC : 2 * C.pairCount <= T.rank) : tensorWeight (contractInvariant T C) = tensorWeight T

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For T of type Ambient Invariant, C of type Index Pairing, and hC of type 2 times C.pair Count < equals T.rank, ambient Tensor Weight ambient Contract Invariant T C equals ambient Tensor Weight T.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientTensorWeight_contractInvariant (T : AmbientInvariant) (C : IndexPairing) (hC : 2 * C.pairCount <= T.rank) : ambientTensorWeight (ambientContractInvariant T C) = ambientTensorWeight T

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Rational scalar multiplication. In explicit terms, for c of type rat, and i of type scalar invariant, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.scalar Mul c I.1.expr rank : equals 0 weight : equals I.1.weight parity : equals I.1.parity, rfl ⟩.

Sum of homogeneous scalar invariants of the same weight. In explicit terms, for i, j of type scalar invariant, and hweight of type i.1.weight equals j.1.weight, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.add I.1.expr J.1.expr rank : equals 0 weight : equals I.1.weight parity : equals Invariant Parity.mixed, rfl ⟩.
correspondence.leanType: def add (I J : ScalarInvariant) (_hWeight : I.1.weight = J.1.weight) : ScalarInvariant

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Base Laplacian, lowering weight by two. In explicit terms, for i of type scalar invariant, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.ambient Laplacian I.1.expr rank : equals 0 weight : equals I.1.weight minus 2 parity : equals I.1.parity, rfl ⟩.

Multiplication by the normalized scalar curvature J. In explicit terms, for i of type scalar invariant, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.j Mul I.1.expr rank : equals 0 weight : equals I.1.weight minus 2 parity : equals I.1.parity, rfl ⟩.

Divergence of a rank-one invariant. In explicit terms, for f of type one form invariant, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.divergence F.1.expr rank : equals 0 weight : equals F.1.weight minus 2 parity : equals F.1.parity, rfl ⟩.

Ambient divergence of a rank-one invariant. In explicit terms, for f of type ambient one form invariant, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.divergence F.1.expr rank : equals 0 weight : equals F.1.weight minus 2 parity : equals F.1.parity, rfl ⟩.

Ambient Laplacian, lowering weight by two. In explicit terms, for i of type ambient scalar invariant, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.ambient Laplacian I.1.expr rank : equals 0 weight : equals I.1.weight minus 2 parity : equals I.1.parity, rfl ⟩.

Restriction of a scalar ambient invariant to the Einstein slice. This is the i^* appearing in Theorem 1.4; it is not restriction to the conformal boundary of the PE manifold. In explicit terms, for i of type ambient scalar invariant, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.restrict Ambient I.1.expr rank : equals 0 weight : equals I.1.weight parity : equals I.1.parity, rfl ⟩.

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
